Brain, heart, lungs missing: Indian sailor dies in Venezuela, family says major organs removed
A shocking discovery has emerged regarding the death of Indian seafarer Rakesh Chauhan in Venezuela. His body, returned to India without vital organs and with extensive prior stitching, has raised grave suspicions. The seafarers' union is demanding a thorough investigation into the alleged foul play and a cover-up, urging immediate intervention from the Indian Embassy for justice and compensation for the grieving family.

Hardik Pandya trade: CSK enter the race; seven franchises express interest
Seven IPL franchises have expressed interest in signing Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder decided to leave Mumbai Indians. Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have joined Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) and Rajasthan Royals in the race, with talks gathering pace. Player consent, leadership opportunities and trade structures are expected to shape one of the biggest IPL moves in recent years.

'Portrayed as Lord Krishna': BJP slams SP for 'mocking Hindus' over Akhilesh's birthday havan
The BJP has strongly criticized the Samajwadi Party, accusing them of disrespecting Hindus after workers depicted Akhilesh Yadav as Lord Krishna during a birthday ritual. This accusation comes amid controversy over alleged Ram Temple donation irregularities. Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath also attacked Yadav, urging him to visit the Ram Temple and recalling past SP actions against devotees.

Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

Beyond coding: What tomorrow’s engineers need to succeed
AI is transforming software engineering, shifting focus from coding speed to higher-value tasks like system design and customer outcomes. Senior leaders at NiCE emphasize that while AI automates code generation, critical thinking, problem framing, and domain expertise are becoming crucial human skills. Young engineers are advised to build strong foundations, stay curious, and focus on business impact to thrive in this evolving landscape.

Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

Who is Shoko Kawata: Why this Japanese mayor's maternity leave is making global headlines
Japan's youngest female mayor, Shoko Kawata, is making waves by becoming the first sitting mayor to take maternity leave. Her decision has ignited a national debate on women in leadership and motherhood, challenging the notion that public office and family life are incompatible. Despite backlash, Kawata insists systems can adapt, not people, advocating for a society where work and family coexist naturally, a sentiment resonating globally with working mothers.
